subject: Anthropology
title: Early Tool Use
description: A short lesson to help you apply Early Tool Use.

hook.text: A sharp stone flake cuts through hide faster than teeth. 2.6 million years ago, someone figured that out.

buildup.text: The oldest known stone tools are Oldowan choppers — simple rocks with edges chipped off by striking.

discovery.text: These tools let early hominins access meat and marrow, opening a high-energy food source that fueled brain growth.

twist.text: We're not sure who made the first tools. It may have been Homo habilis — or even late Australopithecus.

climax.text: Tool use marks a turning point: instead of adapting bodies to the environment, hominins adapted the environment.

punchline.text: The first tool changed the rules of survival.

quiz.question: What are Oldowan tools?
options: Simple stone choppers with chipped edges
